questions.question: What is the form of government of the United States?
What is the supreme law of the land?
Name one thing the U.S. Constitution does.
acceptedAnswers: Protects the rights of the people

questions.question: The U.S. Constitution starts with the words "We the People." What does "We the People" mean?
How are changes made to the U.S. Constitution?
What does the Bill of Rights protect?
acceptedAnswers: (The basic) rights of people living in the United States
distractorOptions: The right to dynamic trade laws
The power of the standing federal army
The right to avoid local tax rules

questions.question: How many amendments does the U.S. Constitution have?
Why is the Declaration of Independence important?
acceptedAnswers: It says America is free from British control.
It says all people are created equal.
distractorOptions: It established the three branches of government.
It created the first U.S. national bank.
It set the exact dynamic terms for senators.

questions.question: What founding document said the American colonies were free from Britain?
Name two important ideas from the Declaration of Independence and the U.S. Constitution.
The words "Life, Liberty, and the pursuit of Happiness" are in what founding document?
What is the economic system of the United States?
What is the rule of law?
acceptedAnswers: No one is above the law.
distractorOptions: Judges can change laws at will.
Only citizens must follow the law.

questions.question: Many documents influenced the U.S. Constitution. Name one.
There are three branches of government. Why?
acceptedAnswers: So one part does not become too powerful
distractorOptions: To make passing federal laws faster
To balance federal and state wage taxes

questions.question: Name the three branches of government.
The President of the United States is in charge of which branch of government?
What part of the federal government writes laws?
What are the two parts of the U.S. Congress?
distractorOptions: The Supreme Court and the House

questions.question: Name one power of the U.S. Congress.
How many U.S. senators are there?
How long is a term for a U.S. senator?
Who is one of your state's U.S. senators now?
acceptedAnswers: Answers will vary. [District of Columbia residents and residents of U.S. territories should answer that D.C. (or the territory where the applicant lives) has no U.S. senators.]
distractorOptions: The current Speaker of the House
The Chief Justice of the Supreme Court

questions.question: How many voting members are in the House of Representatives?
How long is a term for a member of the House of Representatives?
Why do U.S. representatives serve shorter terms than U.S. senators?
acceptedAnswers: To more closely follow public opinion
distractorOptions: To reduce federal spending on campaigns
Because their districts have fewer voters

questions.question: How many senators does each state have?
Why does each state have two senators?
distractorOptions: To match the two major political parties
Because of the state's total land size

questions.question: Name your U.S. representative.
acceptedAnswers: Answers will vary. [Residents of territories with nonvoting Delegates or Resident Commissioners may provide the name of that Delegate or Commissioner. Also acceptable is any statement that the territory has no (voting) representatives in Congress.]

questions.question: What is the name of the Speaker of the House of Representatives now?
acceptedAnswers: Visit uscis.gov/citizenship/testupdates for the name of the Speaker of the House of Representatives.
distractorOptions: The Vice President of the United States
The President pro tempore of the Senate

questions.question: Who does a U.S. senator represent?
distractorOptions: Only the citizens in their specific city

questions.question: Who elects U.S. senators?
Who does a member of the House of Representatives represent?
distractorOptions: The entire population of their state

questions.question: Who elects members of the House of Representatives?
Some states have more representatives than other states. Why?
distractorOptions: Because they pay more federal taxes
Because they joined the Union first

questions.question: The President of the United States is elected for how many years?
The President of the United States can serve only two terms. Why?
acceptedAnswers: To keep the president from becoming too powerful
distractorOptions: Because of an order in the original Articles of Confederation
To guarantee equal time for all parties

questions.question: What is the name of the President of the United States now?
acceptedAnswers: Visit uscis.gov/citizenship/testupdates for the name of the President of the United States.

questions.question: What is the name of the Vice President of the United States now?
acceptedAnswers: Visit uscis.gov/citizenship/testupdates for the name of the Vice President of the United States.

questions.question: If the president can no longer serve, who becomes president?
acceptedAnswers: The Vice President (of the United States)
distractorOptions: The Chief Justice of the Supreme Court

questions.question: Name one power of the president.
Who is Commander in Chief of the U.S. military?
distractorOptions: The Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff

questions.question: Who signs bills to become laws?
distractorOptions: The Chief Justice of the Supreme Court

questions.question: Who vetoes bills?
Who appoints federal judges?
The executive branch has many parts. Name one.
What does the President's Cabinet do?
acceptedAnswers: Advises the President (of the United States)

questions.question: What are two Cabinet-level positions?
acceptedAnswers: Secretary of Health and Human Services
Secretary of Housing and Urban Development
Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency
Administrator of the Small Business Administration
Director of the Central Intelligence Agency
Director of the Office of Management and Budget
distractorOptions: Speaker of the House and Governor

questions.question: Why is the Electoral College important?
acceptedAnswers: It decides who is elected president.
It provides a compromise between the popular election of the president and congressional selection.
distractorOptions: It determines the size of the U.S. House of Representatives.
It handles compliance and education standards.

questions.question: What is one part of the judicial branch?
What does the judicial branch do?
acceptedAnswers: Decides if a law goes against the (U.S.) Constitution

questions.question: What is the highest court in the United States?
How many seats are on the Supreme Court?
How many Supreme Court justices are usually needed to decide a case?
How long do Supreme Court justices serve?
Supreme Court justices serve for life. Why?
distractorOptions: To guarantee consistency in structural formatting
Because they are elected by popular vote

questions.question: Who is the Chief Justice of the United States now?
acceptedAnswers: Visit uscis.gov/citizenship/testupdates for the name of the Chief Justice of the United States.

questions.question: Name one power that is only for the federal government.
Name one power that is only for the states.
What is the purpose of the 10th Amendment?
acceptedAnswers: (It states that the) powers not given to the federal government belong to the states or to the people.
distractorOptions: It guarantees freedom of speech and expression.
It regulates interstate business compliance fields.
It limits the president to serving two full terms.

questions.question: Who is the governor of your state now?
acceptedAnswers: Answers will vary. [District of Columbia residents should answer that D.C. does not have a governor.]
distractorOptions: The Mayor of your local town

questions.question: What is the capital of your state?
acceptedAnswers: Answers will vary. [District of Columbia residents should answer that D.C. is not a state and does not have a capital. Residents of U.S. territories should name the capital of the territory.]
distractorOptions: The largest financial city in your area

questions.question: There are four amendments to the U.S. Constitution about who can vote. Describe one of them.
acceptedAnswers: You don't have to pay (a poll tax) to vote.
Any citizen can vote. (Women and men can vote.)
A male citizen of any race (can vote).
distractorOptions: Only native-born citizens can vote in local elections.
Citizens must own land or commercial properties to vote.
Voting rights are determined directly by state income tax status.

questions.question: Who can vote in federal elections, run for federal office, and serve on a jury in the United States?
distractorOptions: All permanent residents with a visa
Anyone living in a state for over five years

questions.question: What are three rights of everyone living in the United States?
distractorOptions: Free global travel, guaranteed income, and automated legal defense
The right to avoid state taxes, free housing, and dynamic employment
The right to vote in federal elections, run for office, and hold a passport

questions.question: What do we show loyalty to when we say the Pledge of Allegiance?
Name two promises that new citizens make in the Oath of Allegiance.
acceptedAnswers: Give up loyalty to other countries
Obey the laws of the United States
Serve (help, do important work for) the nation (if needed)
Be loyal to the United States
distractorOptions: Register with a political party and run for local office
Pay state compliance fees and buy property
Never leave the physical borders of the country

questions.question: How can people become United States citizens?
acceptedAnswers: Be born in the United States, under the conditions set by the 14th Amendment
Derive citizenship (under conditions set by Congress)
distractorOptions: Apply for a temporary employment visa matching corporate needs
Reside in any U.S. territory for at least six months
Pay an administrative processing fee to a local municipal tax agency

questions.question: What are two examples of civic participation in the United States?
acceptedAnswers: Give an elected official your opinion (on an issue)
Support or oppose an issue or policy
distractorOptions: Watching news cycles and purchasing domestic products
Applying for a driver's license and moving states
Paying mandatory sales tax and managing personal assets

questions.question: What is one way Americans can serve their country?
acceptedAnswers: Work for local, state, or federal government
distractorOptions: Start a solo Micro-SaaS business framework

questions.question: Why is it important to pay federal taxes?
acceptedAnswers: All people pay to fund the federal government
distractorOptions: To purchase local public parks and sloped terrain

questions.question: It is important for all men age 18 through 25 to register for the Selective Service. Name one reason why.
acceptedAnswers: Makes the draft fair, if needed
distractorOptions: To obtain a standard federal passport
To verify regional wage tax tracking updates
To establish automatic voting access details

questions.question: The colonists came to America for many reasons. Name one.
distractorOptions: To avoid traveling to European coastal areas

questions.question: Who lived in America before the Europeans arrived?
What group of people was taken and sold as slaves?
What war did the Americans fight to win independence from Britain?
Name one reason why the Americans declared independence from Britain.
acceptedAnswers: British soldiers stayed in Americans' houses (boarding, quartering)
distractorOptions: The British refused to open public libraries
The King restricted travel to Canada
Disagreements over dynamic industrial assembly lines

questions.question: Who wrote the Declaration of Independence?
When was the Declaration of Independence adopted?
The American Revolution had many important events. Name one.
acceptedAnswers: Washington Crossing the Delaware (Battle of Trenton)

questions.question: There were 13 original states. Name five.
What founding document was written in 1787?
The Federalist Papers supported the passage of the U.S. Constitution. Name one of the writers.
Why were the Federalist Papers important?
acceptedAnswers: They helped people understand the (U.S.) Constitution.
distractorOptions: They criticized high British taxation patterns.
They announced dynamic colonial military alliances.
They outlined the original lines of the Louisiana Purchase.

questions.question: Benjamin Franklin is famous for many things. Name one.
acceptedAnswers: Founded the first free public libraries
First Postmaster General of the United States
Helped write the Declaration of Independence
distractorOptions: First President of the United States
Commanded the Continental Army at Valley Forge

questions.question: George Washington is famous for many things. Name one.
acceptedAnswers: First president of the United States
distractorOptions: Established the First Bank of the United States
Doubled the size of the United States

questions.question: Thomas Jefferson is famous for many things. Name one.
acceptedAnswers: Writer of the Declaration of Independence
Third president of the United States
Doubled the size of the United States (Louisiana Purchase)
Writer of the Virginia Statute on Religious Freedom
distractorOptions: First Postmaster General of the United States
General during the American Civil War

questions.question: James Madison is famous for many things. Name one.
acceptedAnswers: Fourth president of the United States
One of the writers of the Federalist Papers
distractorOptions: Doubled the size of the United States
Wrote the original Star-Spangled Banner lyrics

questions.question: Alexander Hamilton is famous for many things. Name one.
acceptedAnswers: One of the writers of the Federalist Papers
Helped establish the First Bank of the United States
distractorOptions: Third president of the United States

questions.question: What territory did the United States buy from France in 1803?
Name one war fought by the United States in the 1800s.
Name the U.S. war between the North and the South.
The Civil War had many important events. Name one.
Abraham Lincoln is famous for many things. Name one.
acceptedAnswers: Led the United States during the Civil War

questions.question: What did the Emancipation Proclamation do?
acceptedAnswers: Freed slaves in the Confederate states
Freed slaves in most Southern states
distractorOptions: Ended the War of 1812 with Britain
Gave all women the right to vote

questions.question: What U.S. war ended slavery?
What amendment says all persons born or naturalized in the United States, and subject to the jurisdiction thereof, are U.S. citizens?
When did all men get the right to vote?
distractorOptions: When the Declaration of Independence was signed

questions.question: Name one leader of the women's rights movement in the 1800s.
Name one war fought by the United States in the 1900s.
Why did the United States enter World War I?
acceptedAnswers: To support the Allied Powers (England, France, Italy, and Russia)
To oppose the Central Powers (Germany, Austria-Hungary, the Ottoman Empire, and Bulgaria)
distractorOptions: The surprise attack on Pearl Harbor
To stop the spread of communism in Europe
To enforce global corporate compliance structures

questions.question: When did all women get the right to vote?
What was the Great Depression?
acceptedAnswers: Longest economic recession in modern history
distractorOptions: A political dispute before the Civil War
A regional tax collection crisis in Pennsylvania
A global treaty disagreement after World War I

questions.question: When did the Great Depression start?
distractorOptions: The launch of the Interstate Highway system (1956)

questions.question: Who was president during the Great Depression and World War II?
Why did the United States enter World War II?
acceptedAnswers: To support the Allied Powers (England, France, and Russia)
To oppose the Axis Powers (Germany, Italy, and Japan)
distractorOptions: Because Germany attacked U.S. merchant trade lines in 1917
To force Iraqi military forces out of neighboring regions
To support the Central Powers directly

questions.question: Dwight Eisenhower is famous for many things. Name one.
acceptedAnswers: President at the end of (during) the Korean War
34 th president of the United States
Signed the Federal-Aid Highway Act of 1956 (Created the Interstate System)
distractorOptions: First Secretary of State under Washington
Freed the slaves via executive action

questions.question: Who was the United States' main rival during the Cold War?
During the Cold War, what was one main concern of the United States?
Why did the United States enter the Korean War?
acceptedAnswers: To stop the spread of communism
distractorOptions: Because of a surprise attack on civilian ports
To support British colonial trade lines
To adjust regional maritime fishing zones

questions.question: Why did the United States enter the Vietnam War?
acceptedAnswers: To stop the spread of communism
distractorOptions: To retaliate for the bombing of Pearl Harbor
To settle global high-friction trading blockades

questions.question: What did the civil rights movement do?
distractorOptions: Fought for original independence from Britain
Created the first automated labor union laws

questions.question: Martin Luther King, Jr. is famous for many things. Name one.
acceptedAnswers: Worked for equality for all Americans
Worked to ensure that people would "not be judged by the color of their skin, but by the content of their character"
distractorOptions: Served as a General during World War II
Signed the Federal-Aid Highway Act of 1956
Was the first African American president

questions.question: Why did the United States enter the Persian Gulf War?
acceptedAnswers: To force the Iraqi military from Kuwait
distractorOptions: To stop the spread of communism
Because of the attacks on September 11, 2001
To establish independent state-level shipping channels

questions.question: What major event happened on September 11, 2001 in the United States?
acceptedAnswers: Terrorists took over two planes and crashed them into the World Trade Center in New York City
Terrorists took over a plane and crashed into the Pentagon in Arlington, Virginia
Terrorists took over a plane originally aimed at Washington, D.C., and crashed in a field in Pennsylvania
distractorOptions: The U.S. stock market crashed, starting the longest recession
The United States officially entered the Vietnam War
The Civil War between the North and South ended

questions.question: Name one U.S. military conflict after the September 11, 2001 attacks.
Name one American Indian tribe in the United States.
Name one example of an American innovation.
What is the capital of the United States?
Where is the Statue of Liberty?
acceptedAnswers: Liberty Island [Also acceptable are New Jersey, near New York City, and on the Hudson (River).]

questions.question: Why does the flag have 13 stripes?
acceptedAnswers: (Because the stripes) represent the original colonies
distractorOptions: Because there are 13 amendments about voting rights
To honor the 13 signers of the Constitution
Because it was designed on July 13th

questions.question: Why does the flag have 50 stars?
acceptedAnswers: (Because there is) one star for each state
distractorOptions: To represent the first 50 years of independence
To honor the 50 signers of the Declaration
Because there are 50 federal departments and agencies

questions.question: What is the name of the national anthem?
The Nation's first motto was "E Pluribus Unum." What does that mean?
What is Independence Day?
acceptedAnswers: A holiday to celebrate U.S. independence (from Britain)
distractorOptions: A day honoring soldiers who died in service
The day the U.S. Constitution was signed
A holiday celebrating the end of slavery

questions.question: Name three national U.S. holidays.
distractorOptions: Flag Day, Election Day, and Easter
Earth Day, Patriots Day, and Halloween
Lincoln's Birthday, Arbor Day, and Boxing Day

questions.question: What is Memorial Day?
acceptedAnswers: A holiday to honor soldiers who died in military service
distractorOptions: A holiday to honor all individuals currently serving in the military
A celebration of the signing of the peace treaties

questions.question: What is Veterans Day?
acceptedAnswers: A holiday to honor people in the (U.S.) military
A holiday to honor people who have served (in the U.S. military)
distractorOptions: A holiday specifically for soldiers who died in military conflicts
The anniversary day of the American Revolution's conclusion
A civic participation day focused on voting registration
